channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: 3 takeaways from Florida's special election
item.description: Republicans Randy Fine and Jimmy Patronis won special elections in Florida, resulting in the GOP control of the House sitting at a 220-213 margin.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Proposed cut in local aid to prevent aquatic invasive species sparks fears of more infested lakes
item.description: For the past decade, Minnesota has distributed about $10 million a year to counties for aquatic invasive species prevention. Gov. Tim Walz’s proposed budget reduced that amount by 50 percent. Some lake advocates worry cutting the funding could potentially lead to more infested waters.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Courtroom combat: Inside the federal judiciary system where Trump's agenda is under assault
item.description: More than 120 lawsuits are challenging Trump’s second-term agenda. Here’s how the federal court system works and how it’s become ground zero in the lawfare against his agenda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: Susan Crawford, a Dane County judge who led legal fights to protect union power and abortion rights and to oppose voter ID, defeated Republican-backed Brad Schimel in a race that broke records for spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate Republicans set to bypass parliamentarian on Trump tax cuts
item.description: Republicans are set to make the audacious play of bypassing the Senate parliamentarian and moving forward with a budget resolution based on a scoring baseline set by Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How couriers changed the playing field of the Texas Lottery
item.description: Lottery couriers have gained newfound scrutiny in Texas after Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick raised questions about the use of a courier to sell an $83.5 million jackpot ticket.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: State Sen. Napoleon Harris wins Thornton Township supervisor seat, Jason House victorious in Dolton mayoral race
item.description: Village of Dolton Senior Trustee Jason House won the election for Dolton mayor by a landslide, while State Sen. Napoleon B. Harris III claimed victory to become the Thornton Township Supervisor-elect, unseating the incumbent, self-proclaimed “Super Mayor” Tiffany Henyard.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, WGN 9

item.title: New York City mayoral candidates vow to address rising antisemitism
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The leading candidates for New York City mayor say they have plans to fight a rise in antisemitism. One in 10 New Yorkers is Jewish, making it the largest Jewish population outside of Israel. Former Gov.
item.dc:creator: Anthony DiLorenzo, PIX 11

item.title: Naval Academy removes nearly 400 books from library in new DEI purge ordered by Hegseth's office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Naval Academy has removed nearly 400 books from its library after being told by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's office to review and get rid of ones that promote diversity, equity and inclusion, U.S officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Liberal wins first major 2025 statewide battleground election in race turned into Trump-Musk referendum
item.description: The liberal candidate won in the first major statewide battleground election of 2025, protecting the progressive majority on the Wisconsin's supreme court, in a race that became a referendum on President Donald Trump and Elon Musk, and broke records as the most expense court election in the nat
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CCSD opposes school choice bill, while lawmakers say it empowers families
item.description: The Clark County School District is against a bill that would let parents enroll their children at any public school regardless of where they live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Speaker Johnson suffers a defeat in his push to block parents in the U.S. House from proxy voting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Speaker Mike Johnson endured a decisive defeat Tuesday after he staged an unusually aggressive effort to squash a proposal for new parents in Congress to able to vote by proxy, rather than in person, as they care for newborns.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top scientists warn that Trump policies are causing a ‘climate of fear’ in research
item.description: Some 2,000 scientists, including dozens of Nobel Prize winners, have signed an open letter warning that the U.S. lead in science is being “decimated” by the Trump administration’s cuts to research.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: New Jersey Sen. Booker presses his marathon speech against Trump's agenda past 20 hours and counting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Sen. Cory Booker held the Senate floor with a marathon speech that lasted all night and into Tuesday afternoon in a feat of endurance to show Democrats’ objections to President Donald Trump’s sweeping actions.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Layoffs begin at U.S. health agencies responsible for research, tracking disease and regulating food
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department began receiving notices of dismissal Tuesday in an overhaul ultimately expected to lay off up to 10,000 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Missouri pastor gets deportation notice for sponsored son from Haiti
item.description: "Do we welcome immigrants in, if they come legally, and are pursing the citizenship process? Is that true or is that not?"
item.dc:creator: Hannah King, Fox 5

item.title: White House says it’s ’case closed’ on the Signal group chat review
item.description: Last week, the White House said the National Security Council, the White House counsel office and President Trump adviser Elon Musk were all looking into the mishap.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Trump steps up efforts to exert control over DC
item.description: President Trump is growing more aggressive in his posture towards Washington, D.C, threatening to exert more control over the local affairs of the nation's capital.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds protest the ICE detainment of a U of M graduate student
item.description: Protesters gathered at the University of Minnesota Twin Cities campus after an international graduate student was detained by immigration officials on Thursday. During the rally, it was announced that a student at Minnesota State University, Mankato was also detained.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Matt Sepic, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illinois to defy Trump voter order for April election
item.description: President Trump earlier this week issued an executive order calling for all voters to provide proof of citizenship before receiving a ballot.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: California governor candidate Chad Bianco says he'd abolish the state income tax if elected
item.description: Citing a desire to “restore the California Dream for all,” a Republican candidate in the 2026 race to become California’s next governor says he’d eliminate the state’s income tax if elected.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Per-pupil funding going up $2 next year unless something changes in Nevada budget
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's proposed state budget sets per-pupil funding in 2026 at $9,416, just $2 higher than the current funding level. Going higher than that is "impracticable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida House proposes largest sales tax cut in state history
item.description: The Florida House of Representatives is proposing the largest cut to sales tax in state history, from 6% to 5.25%.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5
